checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=9,261,878.25 (2015-12-31) rising to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=21,350,911.70 (2016-03-31), OtherAssetsNoncurrent=33,589,517.79 (2016-06-30), OtherAssetsNoncurrent=36,119,852.69 (2016-09-30), then collapsing to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=7,515,830.91 (2016-12-31)
Goodwill=17,677,469.91 (2014-06-30) increasing to Goodwill=48,426,657.70 (2015-03-31), a +174% jump within three quarters versus modest revenue growth in the same window

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=28,842,718.00 (FY2015) plus DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization=58,369,750.15 (FY2015) implies ~87.2M non-cash-adjusted earnings vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=34,537,327.77 (FY2015), a gap of roughly $52.7M

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold last reported value is for period 2010-07-01 to 2011-06-30 (value=2,741,131.57); no CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold data provided for FY2012 through FY2016 despite Revenues data continuing through 2016-12-31

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss declines sharply from 12,017,734.58 (2014-04-01 to 2014-06-30) to 1,257,805.07 (2016-04-01 to 2016-06-30) and then to -2,210,264.98 (2016-07-01 to 2016-09-30) and -1,181,856.27 (2016-10-01 to 2016-12-31), while quarterly Revenues remain comparatively stable in the 196M-213M range over the same period

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=780,412.63 (2012-06-30) versus AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=776,537.70 (2015-06-30) -- essentially flat -- while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent nearly doubled from 52,400,794.73 (2012-06-30) to 101,439,692.72 (2015-06-30)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2012-05-18 followed by two 10-Q/A filings on 2012-05-25 (for periods 2011-09-30 and 2011-12-31), all filed roughly 5-8 months after the original filings
10-K/A filing_date=2015-03-30 amending prior annual report; NT 10-Q filing_date=2017-02-09 filed one day before the actual 10-Q filing_date=2017-02-10

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold data ends at period 2010-07-01 to 2011-06-30, and AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent data ends at 2014-09-30, leaving multi-year gaps for margin and liability-growth analysis despite Revenues/Assets data continuing through 2016-12-31

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition combined with under-reserved receivables: receivables grew materially faster than revenue in FY2013 (+14.8% vs +2.4%) and FY2014 (+36.5% vs +23.7%), while the doubtful-account allowance stayed nearly flat relative to a near-doubling of receivables from 2012-06-30 to 2015-06-30, suggesting collectibility risk was not being reserved commensurately with receivable growth.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=780,412.63 (2012-06-30) vs 776,537.70 (2015-06-30) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent nearly doubled over the same window

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Earnings quality deterioration where reported net income is not supported by cash generation, consistent with aggressive accrual recognition, deferred expense recognition, or working-capital-driven cash consumption not fully explained by AR/deferred revenue movements; culminates in FY2016 where positive net income was paired with negative operating cash flow from continuing operations.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=28,842,718.00 (FY2015) with DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization=58,369,750.15 (FY2015) implying ~87.2M cash-adjusted earnings vs actual NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=34,537,327.77 (FY2015)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible improper capitalization of costs into 'other assets' during 2016 followed by a large subsequent write-off/reclassification (value fell from ~$36.1M at 2016-09-30 to ~$7.5M at 2016-12-31), which would defer expense recognition into a later period and temporarily inflate reported assets/earnings.

top_signals: Cluster of 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments filed 2012-05-18/2012-05-25 covering FY2011 annual and two FY2012 quarterly periods, plus another 10-K/A in 2015-03-30, indicating a history of restated financials
Persistent and widening divergence between NetIncomeLoss and operating cash flow, culminating in FY2016 negative operating cash flow (-$4.04M) despite $19.25M reported net income
Accounts receivable growing materially faster than revenue in FY2013 (+14.8% vs +2.4%) and FY2014 (+36.5% vs +23.7%) alongside a flat doubtful-account allowance
Sharp spike-then-collapse pattern in OtherAssetsNoncurrent during 2016 (from ~$9.3M to ~$36.1M then down to ~$7.5M within a year), suggestive of capitalization/reclassification issues
NT 10-Q filed immediately (one day) before the actual 10-Q, indicating filing timeliness stress
